---
title: "PART A"
author: "MIKE"
date: "2026-02-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

### Reading Data and Data Cleaning

```{r}
my_data = read.csv("tele.csv")
str(my_data)

# Add new flag of whether the customer was contacted
#my_data$was_contacted = ifelse(my_data$pdays == 999, 0, 1)

## ciao
# random line
#test2
removes = c("X", "duration", "pdays") # duration is unknown when calling
to_be_factors = c("job", "marital", "education", "default", "housing", "loan", "contact", "month", "day_of_week", "poutcome", "y")

my_data[removes] = NULL
my_data[to_be_factors] = lapply(my_data[to_be_factors], factor)


summary(my_data)
```
# convert binary
```{r}
# Identify binary factors (2 levels: "yes"/"no" or "no"/"unknown")
binary_factors <- sapply(my_data, function(x) is.factor(x) && nlevels(x) == 2)

# Convert to 0/1 numeric (yes=1, no=0)
for(col in names(my_data)[binary_factors]) {
  my_data[[col]] <- ifelse(my_data[[col]] == "yes", 1, 0)
}

# Verify
str(my_data[, names(my_data)[binary_factors]])
```

# equation verification

```{r}
success_rate <- 0.12
baseline <- 0.11

retention <- 1000+((success_rate - baseline)*10000)
#print(retention)
whole_training_cost <- 1000

training_cost_per_call <- whole_training_cost/retention

print(training_cost_per_call)
```



### Split Data into training and testing

```{r}
ratio = 0.7
set.seed(12345) 
train_rows = sample(1:nrow(my_data), ratio*nrow(my_data))
training = my_data[train_rows, ]
testing = my_data[-train_rows, ]
```

### Logistic model creation

```{r}
# what is y? 
linear_model = glm(y ~ ., data = training, family = binomial)
#stepmodel = step(linear_model, direction = "backward", trace = 0)
### Logistic model creation 


full_stepmodel = step(linear_model, direction = "backward")
saveRDS(full_stepmodel, file = "full_stepmodel_without_pdays.rds")
loaded_full_stepmodel <- readRDS("full_stepmodel_without_pdays.rds")

library(caret)
pred_class <- predict(loaded_full_stepmodel, testing, type = "response")
pred_factor <- ifelse(pred_class > 0.2, "yes", "no")
pred_factor <- factor(pred_factor, levels = levels(training$y))

confusionMatrix(pred_factor, testing$y, positive = "yes")

```



# fine tuning
```{r}
library(caret)

# Get predictions once
pred_probs <- predict(loaded_full_stepmodel, testing, type = "response")

# Define levels from your data (this was missing)
y_levels <- levels(training$y)  # or levels(testing$y)

# Initialize tracking
best_profit <- -Inf
best_thresh <- NULL
best_cm <- NULL

# Loop over thresholds 0.00 to 1.00
for(thresh in seq(0.00, 1.00, by = 0.01)) {
  # Classify predictions
  sorted_pred <- ifelse(pred_probs >= thresh, "yes", "no")
  pred_factor <- factor(sorted_pred, levels = y_levels)
  
  # Confusion matrix
  cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_factor, testing$y, positive = "yes")
  
  # Extract counts from table
  tbl <- cm$table
  TP <- as.numeric(tbl[2,2])  # Predicted Yes, Actual Yes
  FP <- as.numeric(tbl[1,2])  # Predicted Yes, Actual No  
  FN <- as.numeric(tbl[2,1])  # Predicted No, Actual Yes
  
  # Calculate profit: 10*TP - 2*(FP + FN)
  profit <- 10 * TP - 2 * (FP + FN)
  
  # Track best
  if(profit > best_profit) {
    best_profit <- profit
    best_thresh <- thresh
    best_cm <- cm
  }
}

# Results
cat("BEST PROFIT:", round(best_profit, 2), "at threshold:", best_thresh, "\n")
print(best_cm)
cat("Calls made (FP+TP):", best_cm$table[2,1] + best_cm$table[2,2], "\n")

```

# clustering

```{r}
clustering_telemarketing <- as.data.frame(model.matrix(~.-1, data = my_data))

minmax <- function(x){
	(x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x))
}

int.minmax <- as.data.frame(lapply(clustering_telemarketing, minmax))

set.seed(12345)
clustering_dummy <- kmeans(int.minmax, 5)
clustering_dummy$size
clustering_dummy$centers

clustering_telemarketing$ClusterNumber <- clustering_dummy$cluster
tapply(clustering_telemarketing$y,clustering_telemarketing$ClusterNumber, mean)
# it seems that cluster 4 has the highest call success rate (26%)

tapply(clustering_telemarketing$age,clustering_telemarketing$ClusterNumber, mean)


```
# clustering visualization
```{r}


# Identify binaries (0/1 vars)
binary_vars <- sapply(features, function(x) length(unique(x[!is.na(x)])) == 2 && 
                      all(unique(x[!is.na(x)]) %in% c(0,1)))
continuous_vars <- names(features)[!binary_vars]

# Then plot separately
for(var in names(features)[binary_vars]) {
  barplot(tapply(clustering_telemarketing[[var]], clustering_telemarketing$ClusterNumber, mean),
          main = var, ylab = "Proportion")
}
for(var in continuous_vars) {
  boxplot(clustering_telemarketing[[var]] ~ clustering_telemarketing$ClusterNumber,
          main = var)
}
library(ggplot2); library(reshape2)
data_long <- melt(clustering_telemarketing[, c(names(features), "ClusterNumber")], 
                  id.vars = "ClusterNumber")

ggplot(data_long, aes(x = factor(ClusterNumber), y = value)) +
  facet_wrap(~ Variable, scales = "free") +
  geom_boxplot(data = subset(data_long, !Variable %in% names(features)[binary_vars])) +
  geom_col(data = subset(data_long, Variable %in% names(features)[binary_vars]), 
           stat = "summary", fun = "mean", fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Cluster Profiles: Boxplots (Continuous) + Means (Binary)")


```




### Make predictions

```{r}
prob = predict(stepmodel, testing, type = "response")
pred = ifelse(prob >= 0.5, "yes", "no")
pred = factor(pred, levels = levels(testing$y))
```

### Evaluation

```{r}
library(caret)
confusionMatrix(pred, testing$y, positive = "yes")

```
